title: Adenosma capitata
intro: Adenosma capitata, also known as Adenosma or Common Adenosma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in reducing symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Adenosma capitata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Adenosma capitata is commonly used to make infusions and decoctions, as well as being dried and used in the form of capsules or tablets.
taxonomy: Adenosma capitata is classified within the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. This family consists of approximately 1,844 species across 181 genera. Adenosma capitata is a member of the tribe Boragininae, which is a subtribe within the family. The genus Adenosma is part of this subtribe, and it contains several species. The classification of Adenosma capitata is further refined by its species-specific characteristics, such as its geographical distribution and morphological features.
